operator_instances.transformation_goal: Enhance data complexity by allowing rows with missing cells (shorter than header length), requiring bounds checking.
operator_instances.rationale: The original seed assumes rows always have all columns. Real proteomics data may have missing values. The function must handle out-of-bounds access gracefully.

data_state_patch.description_note: CSV rows may have missing cells; treat missing values as empty strings.

new_hidden_tests.description: Row with missing Charge column (shorter by 1) should default Charge to '0'.
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
mapRow(row, header_dict, precursors_mapping, sequences_mapping, protein_mapping)
print(precursors_mapping, sequences_mapping, protein_mapping)

expected_failure_modes: IndexError if row is shorter than index

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Test that function handles missing cell gracefully.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: Function must not raise IndexError; treat missing as empty string.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: Seed gold solution without bounds check would raise IndexError on missing cell.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Row has one fewer cell than header; function should default missing to empty string.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include tests with rows missing optional columns.

operator_instances.transformation_goal: Add computational constraints: trim whitespace from peptide_name and charge_state; validate charge is integer or default to '0'.
operator_instances.rationale: The seed solution does not trim whitespace or validate charge. Real data may have extra spaces, requiring robust parsing.

data_state_patch.description_note: Values may include leading/trailing whitespace; charge must be integer or default to '0'.

new_hidden_tests.description: Charge with whitespace ' 2 ' should be trimmed and treated as '2'; non-integer 'abc' defaults to '0'.
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
mapRow(row, header_dict, precursors_mapping, sequences_mapping, protein_mapping)
print('PEP/2' in precursors_mapping)

expected_failure_modes: Untrimmed whitespace causes key mismatch; non-integer charge used as key literally

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Test that function trims whitespace and validates charge.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: peptide_name and charge_state must be stripped; if charge not valid integer (after stripping), default to '0'.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: Seed gold solution would not trim and would keep ' 2 ' as charge, leading to key 'PEP/ 2 ' instead of 'PEP/2'.

input_variant.value: row with whitespace in peptide and charge

semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Row with leading/trailing whitespace and charge string ' 2 '.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must test trimming and charge validation.

operator_instances.transformation_goal: Increase adversarial robustness: avoid duplicate entries in sequences_mapping; handle extra keys in header_dict gracefully.
operator_instances.rationale: The seed solution may add duplicate keys to sequences_mapping if same peptide/charge appears in multiple rows. Also, header_dict may contain extra columns that should be ignored. These are common adversarial scenarios in real-world data pipelines.

data_state_patch.description_note: Rows may be repeated; header_dict may contain extra keys not in the problem spec.

new_hidden_tests.description: Process same row twice; sequences_mapping should not contain duplicate keys in list.
new_hidden_tests.call_code:
mapRow(row, header_dict, precursors_mapping, sequences_mapping, protein_mapping)
mapRow(row, header_dict, precursors_mapping, sequences_mapping, protein_mapping)
print(len(sequences_mapping['PEPTIDE']), sequences_mapping['PEPTIDE'])

expected_failure_modes: Duplicate keys in sequences_mapping; KeyError if extra header key used incorrectly

semantic_test_specs.semantic_intent: Test that sequences_mapping entries are unique per peptide.
semantic_test_specs.target_constraint: For each peptide, the list in sequences_mapping must not contain duplicate keys.
semantic_test_specs.expected_failure_mode: Seed gold solution uses extend() without checking for existing keys, causing duplicates.
semantic_test_specs.test_case_description: Two consecutive calls with identical row; sequences_mapping list should have exactly two unique entries.

output_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include repeated row processing to test dedup.
